name: HMS Warwick Deeping (FY 182)
events: 18 Oct 1939 HMS H 43 (Lt.Cdr. W.A.K.N. Cavaye, RN) shifted from Portsmouth to Portland. She was escorted by HMS Warwick Deeping (Lt.Cdr. K.M. Drake, RNR) (1)

5 Oct 1940 Around 1030 A/5, HMS Swordfish (Lt. P.J. Cowell, RN) ended her 10th war patrol at Portsmouth. She, and HMS Utmost (Lt. J.H. Eaden, DSC, RN), were escorted in by HMS Warwick Deeping (Skr. J.R. Bruce, RNR). (2)
7 Oct 1940 At 1045 A/7, HMS Ursula (Lt.Cdr. W.A.K.N. Cavaye, RN) ended her 12th war patrol at Portsmouth (Gosport, Haslar Creek, Fort Blockhouse, HMS Dolphin).
She was escorted in by the A/S trawler HMS Warwick Deeping (Skr. J.R. Bruce, RNR) which had joined around 0655 A/7. (3)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
